title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
summary: Flash is a deployment framework that reduces GPU cloud iteration cycles by replacing Docker build-push-deploy workflows with a single @flash.endpoint decorator on Python functions. Hot reload enables model swaps without container rebuilds, and integrates with RunPod's GPU cloud infrastructure.
writeup: This talk introduces Flash, a deployment framework from RunPod that aims to shorten GPU cloud iteration cycles by replacing the Docker build-push-deploy loop with a single @flash.endpoint decorator applied to Python functions. The core claim is that deployment can happen from within the IDE, without manual container steps. Key takeaways: hot reload allows model swaps without container rebuilds, and the framework integrates with RunPod's GPU cloud infrastructure for serverless inference.
